subject: Why I left my career to sell beef
preheader: The story behind FarmThru, from the person who started it.
body:
I want to tell you why FarmThru exists. The real reason.

A few years ago, I was standing in a supermarket holding a tray of beef mince. It was grey around the edges. The label said "Product of Australia" but nothing else. No farm name. No region. No idea how it was raised or how long it had been sitting there.

I had three kids at home. I was feeding them this every week. And I couldn't answer the most basic question: where did this come from?

That was the moment. Not a business plan on a whiteboard. Just a parent, in a supermarket, feeling like something was off.

I started visiting farms. Rachel Ward's property in Kempsey. Bundarra Berkshires up near Bundarra. Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast. These farmers were producing incredible food on regenerative land, and the big chains were paying them barely enough to survive.

So we built FarmThru. A grocery store that connects these farms directly to families through local collection hubs. No warehouses. No wholesalers. Just paddock to pick-up in 2-3 days.

Today we work with 50+ farms across NSW. More than 2,000 families collect from our hubs every week. And Rachel became our co-founder because she believed in what we were building.

I'm not going to pretend this is easy. Building a new kind of grocery store is hard. But every time a family collects their order and says they can taste the difference, I know we're on the right track.

More soon.

Jeremy
creative_brief: Personal founder email. Plain text style, minimal formatting. Small headshot of Jeremy in the signature. Single CTA button to the farm partners page. Should feel like a genuine letter, not a marketing email.
